questions.questionText:
If momentum (P), area (A) and time (T) are
taken to be the fundamental quantities then the
dimensional formula for energy is
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
Let [E] = K[P]x[A]y [T]z

[ML2T–2] = [MLT–1]x[L2]y[T]z

[ML2T–2] = [Mx][Lx+2y][T–x+z]

Comparing both side we get,

x = 1

x + 2y = 2

$$ \Rightarrow $$ 1 + 2y= 2 or y = $$\frac{1}{2} $$

z – x = –2 $$ \Rightarrow $$ z–1 = –2 or z = –1

$$ \therefore $$ [E] = $$\left[ {P{A^{{1 \over 2}}}{T^{ - 1}}} \right]$$
questions.instruction: Choose the correct option out of the four choices.

questions.questionText:
The least count of the main scale of a vernier
callipers is 1 mm. Its vernier scale is divided
into 10 divisions and coincide with 9 divisions
of the main scale. When jaws are touching
each other, the 7<sup>th</sup> division of vernier scale
coincides with a division of main scale and the
zero of vernier scale is lying right side of the
zero of main scale. When this vernier is used to
measure length of a cylinder the zero of the
vernier scale between 3.1 cm and 3.2 cm and
4<sup>th</sup> VSD coincides with a main scale division.
The length of the cylinder is : (VSD is vernier
scale division)
questions.subject: Physics
questions.instruction: Choose the correct option out of the four choices.

questions.questionText:
If speed V, area A and force F are chosen as
fundamental units, then the dimension of
Young’s modulus will be
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
Y = k [F]x [A]y [V]z

[M1L1T –2] = [MLT–2]x [L2]y [LT–1]z

[M1L1T –2] = [M]x [L]x+2y+z[T]–2x–z

Comparing power of M, L and T

x = 1 ……(1)

x + 2y + z = –1 ……(2)

–2x – z = –2 ……(3)

After solving

x = 1

y = –1

z = 0

$$ \therefore $$ Y = FA–1V0
questions.instruction: Choose the correct option out of the four choices.

questions.questionText:
Amount of solar energy received on the earth’s surface per unit area per unit time is defined a solar
constant. Dimension of solar constant is :
questions.subject: Physics
questions.instruction: Choose the correct option out of the four choices.

questions.questionText:
Using screw gauge of pitch 0.1 cm and
50 divisions on its circular scale, the thickness
of an object is measured. It should correctly be
recorded as
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
Using a screw gauge of pitch 0.1 cm and 50 divisions on its circular scale, the thickness of an object is measured as:

Measurement = (Main scale reading) + (Circular scale reading × Least count)

where the least count is calculated as the pitch of the screw gauge divided by the number of divisions on the circular scale:

Least count = (Pitch of screw gauge) / (Number of circular scale divisions)

Least count = $${{0.1} \over {50}}$$ = 0.002 cm

Now if we multiply division of circular scale with least count then we get 0th digit of fraction part even.

Here only option B has 0th digit of fraction part even.
questions.instruction: Choose the correct option out of the four choices.

questions.questionText:
A quantity x is given by $$\left( {{{IF{v^2}} \over {W{L^4}}}} \right)$$ in terms of moment of inertia I, force F, velocity v, work W and
Length L. The dimensional formula for x is same as that of :
questions.subject: Physics
questions.instruction: Choose the correct option out of the four choices.

questions.questionText: Dimensional formula for thermal conductivity is (here K denotes the temperature):
questions.subject: Physics
questions.instruction: Choose the correct option out of the four choices.

questions.questionText:
The quantities x = $${1 \over {\sqrt {{\mu _0}{\varepsilon _0}} }}$$, y = $${E \over B}$$ and z = $${l \over {CR}}$$ are
defined where C-capacitance, R-Resistance, l-length, E-Electric field, B-magnetic field and $${{\varepsilon _0}}$$, $${{\mu _0}}$$, - free space permittivity and permeability respectively. Then :
options: Only y and z have the same dimension
x, y and z have the same dimension
Only x and y have the same dimension
Only x and z have the same dimension
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
x = $${1 \over {\sqrt {{\mu _0}{\varepsilon _0}} }}$$ = speed

$$ \therefore $$ [x] = [L1T–1]

y = $${E \over B}$$ = speed

$$ \therefore $$ [y] = [L1T–1]

z = $${l \over {CR}}$$ = $${l \over \tau }$$

$$ \Rightarrow $$ [z] = [L1T–1]

So, x, y, z all have the same dimensions.
questions.instruction: Choose the correct option out of the four choices.

questions.questionText:
A physical quantity z depends on four observables
a, b, c and d, as z = $${{{a^2}{b^{{2 \over 3}}}} \over {\sqrt c {d^3}}}$$. The percentages of error in the measurement of a, b, c and d are 2%, 1.5%, 4% and 2.5% respectively. The percentage of error in z is :
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
z = $${{{a^2}{b^{{2 \over 3}}}} \over {\sqrt c {d^3}}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow $$ $${{dz} \over z} \times 100 = \left( {2{{da} \over a} + {2 \over 3}{{db} \over b} + {1 \over 2}{{dc} \over c} + 3{{d\left( d \right)} \over d}} \right) \times 100$$

% error in z

= $$\left( {2 \times 2 + {2 \over 3} \times 1.5 + {1 \over 2} \times 4 + 3 \times 2.5} \right) $$ %

= ( 4 + 1 + 2 + 7.5 ) %

= 14.5 %
questions.instruction: Choose the correct option out of the four choices.

questions.questionText:
A student measuring the diameter of a pencil of circular cross-section with the help of a vernier scale records the following four readings 5.50 mm, 5.55 mm, 5.45 mm, 5.65 mm. The average of these four readings is 5.5375 mm and the standard deviation of the data is 0.07395 mm. The average diameter
of the pencil should therefore be recorded as :
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
Given, dav = 5.5375 mm

$$\Delta $$d = 0.07395 mm

Significant rule says that reading should has same significant figure as that of reading given.

$$ \because $$ Measured data are up to two digits after decimal.

$$ \therefore $$ 5.5375 rounded to $$ \to $$ 5.54
questions.instruction: Choose the correct option out of the four choices.

questions.questionText:
A screw gauge has 50 divisions on its circular scale. The circular scale is 4 units ahead of the
pitch scale marking, prior to use. Upon one complete rotation of the circular scale, a displacement of 0.5mm is noticed on the pitch scale. The nature of zero error involved and the least
count of the screw gauge, are respectively :
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
Least count of screw gauge

= $${{0.5} \over {50}}$$

= 1 $$ \times $$ 10-5 m

= 10 $$\mu $$m

Zero error in positive.
questions.instruction: Choose the correct option out of the four choices.

questions.questionText:
The density of a solid metal sphere is determined by measuring its mass and its diameter. The
maximum error in the density of the sphere is $$\left( {{x \over {100}}} \right)$$ %.  If the relative errors in measuring the mass
and the diameter are 6.0% and 1.5% respectively, the value of x is_______.
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
$$\rho $$ = $${M \over V}$$ = $${M \over {{4 \over 3}\pi {{\left( {{D \over 2}} \right)}^3}}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow $$ $$\rho $$ = $${6 \over \pi }M{D^{ - 3}}$$

For maximum error

$${{d\rho } \over \rho } \times 100 = {{dM} \over M} \times 100 + 3{{dD} \over D} \times 100$$

= 6 + 3 $$ \times $$ 1.5

= 10.5 %

= $$\left( {{{1050} \over {100}}} \right)$$ %

$$ \therefore $$ x = 1050

questions.questionText:
The dimension of $${{{B^2}} \over {2{\mu _0}}}$$, where B is magnetic field and $${{\mu _0}}$$
 is the magnetic permeability of vacuum,
is :
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
As $${{{B^2}} \over {2{\mu _0}}}$$ = Energy per unit volume

$$ \therefore $$ Dimension = $${{M{L^2}{T^{ - 2}}} \over {{L^3}}}$$ = ML-1T–2
questions.instruction: Choose the correct option out of the four choices.

questions.questionText: The dimension of stopping potential V<sub>0</sub> in photoelectric effect in units of Planck's constant 'h', speed of light 'c' and Gravitational constant 'G' and ampere A is :
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
V0 $$ \propto $$ hPcQGRIS

[V0] = [M1L2T–3A–1]

[c] = [L1T–1]

[h] = [M1L2T–1]

[G] = [M–1L3T–2]

[I] = [A]

$$ \therefore $$ [M1L2T–3A–1] = [MP–R L2P+Q+3R T–P–Q–2R AS]

Comparing dimension of M, L, T, A, we get

P – R = 1 ; 2P + Q + 3R = 2

– P – Q – 2R = – 3 ; S = – 1

$$ \Rightarrow $$ P = 0, Q = 5, R = –1, S = –1

$$ \therefore $$ V0 $$ \propto $$ h0 c5 G-1 A-1
questions.instruction: Choose the correct option out of the four choices.

questions.questionText:
For the four sets of three measured physical quantities as given below. Which of the following options is correct ?
(i) A1 = 24.36, B1 = 0.0724, C1 = 256.2
(ii) A2 = 24.44, B2 = 16.082, C2 = 240.2
(iii) A3 = 25.2, B3 = 19.2812, C3 = 236.183
(iv) A4 = 25, B4 = 236.191, C4 = 19.5
options:
A<sub>1</sub> + B<sub>1</sub> + C<sub>1</sub> = A<sub>2</sub> + B<sub>2</sub> + C<sub>2</sub> = A<sub>3</sub> + B<sub>3</sub> + C<sub>3</sub>
= A<sub>4</sub> + B<sub>4</sub> + C<sub>4</sub>

A<sub>4</sub> + B<sub>4</sub> + C<sub>4</sub> $$ < $$ A<sub>1</sub> + B<sub>1</sub> + C<sub>1</sub> $$ < $$ A<sub>3</sub> + B<sub>3</sub> + C<sub>3</sub>
$$ < $$ A<sub>2</sub> + B<sub>2</sub> + C<sub>2</sub>

A<sub>4</sub> + B<sub>4</sub> + C<sub>4</sub> $$ < $$ A<sub>1</sub> + B<sub>1</sub> + C<sub>1</sub> = A<sub>2</sub> + B<sub>2</sub> + C<sub>2</sub>
= A<sub>3</sub> + B<sub>3</sub> + C<sub>3</sub>

A<sub>4</sub> + B<sub>4</sub> + C<sub>4</sub> $$ > $$ A<sub>3</sub> + B<sub>3</sub> + C<sub>3</sub> = A<sub>2</sub> + B<sub>2</sub> + C<sub>2</sub> $$ > $$
A<sub>1</sub> + B<sub>1</sub> + C<sub>1</sub>
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
A1 + B1 + C1 = 24.36 + 0.0724 + 256.2
= 280.6324 = 280.6 (After rounding off)

A2 + B2 + C2 = 24.44 + 16.082 + 240.2
= 280.722 = 280.7 (After rounding off)

A3 + B3 + C3 = 25.2 + 19.2812 + 236.183
= 280.6642 = 280.7 (After rounding off)

A4 + B4 + C4 = 25 + 236.191 + 19.5
= 280.691 = 281 (After rounding off)
questions.instruction: Choose the correct option out of the four choices.
